twitter.hook: On December 8, 1994, Jacobo Grinberg-Zylberbaum vanished from his home near Mexico City. His computers and research files were removed from his properties. The lead investigator who challenged the prime suspect's account was subsequently expelled from the police force. 32 years later: no body, no resolution.
thread: Grinberg was a UNAM neurophysiologist — PhD from UNAM, postdoc at NYU. In 1987 he founded the Instituto Nacional para el Estudio de la Conciencia: one of the first institutional consciousness research programs in Latin America. He published dozens of peer-reviewed papers and 50+ books.

The experiment: two meditators placed in separate electromagnetically shielded Faraday chambers. No physical connection. No audio or visual signal between them. After a period of 'connection,' they were isolated. One subject was shown a random visual stimulus.

The paired subject — completely isolated — showed a correlated EEG response. Published correlation: p < 0.005. The paper appeared in Physics Essays in 1994, co-authored with quantum physicist Amit Goswami, and proposed consciousness connects through a non-local field Grinberg called 'the Lattice.'

The most rigorous replication: Leanna Standish at Bastyr University (2004), using fMRI rather than EEG. Correlated brain activity between physically isolated pairs at statistically significant levels. A 2005 Radin meta-analysis of 25 similar studies found a modest but consistent combined effect size — not a methodological artifact signature.

The CIA connection: parts of Grinberg's transferred-potential work appear in declassified CIA documents related to Project Stargate. His work was known, read, and filed by American agencies studying non-local consciousness. He was aware of Stargate and referenced it in his own writing.

December 8, 1994: Grinberg left his home in Tepoztlán. His computers were removed. His research materials were removed. His wife was named as a suspect. The lead investigator who challenged her account was expelled from the force. No body has been found. No credible sighting. 32 years.

We can say: correlated EEG between isolated subjects at p < 0.005. We can say: Bastyr replication using fMRI produced consistent results. We can say: his work appears in CIA files. We can't say: why he disappeared or what the CIA wanted.
Something happened. Someone wanted his work gone. That's not a conspiracy theory. That's the documented record.
twitter.cta: The full case file — the experiments, the CIA documents, the disappearance: https://www.blackswanp.com/research/scientists/jacobo-grinberg — Share if you think the disappearance and the research are connected.

linkedin.hook:
In 1994, a UNAM neurophysiologist published a peer-reviewed paper showing EEG correlations between meditators in separate Faraday cages — p < 0.005.

Four months later, he disappeared. His research files were removed from his properties. The investigator who challenged the prime suspect was expelled from the force.

32 years later, the case is officially unsolved.
linkedin.body:
Jacobo Grinberg-Zylberbaum is one of the most unusual figures in the history of consciousness research — and one of the most systematically ignored.

His credentials were real: PhD in neurophysiology from UNAM, postdoctoral position at NYU, founder of the Instituto Nacional para el Estudio de la Conciencia in 1987. He published in peer-reviewed journals, authored more than 50 books, and ran experimental protocols that most Western neuroscience departments refused to consider.

His most consequential study: two meditators placed in separate electromagnetically shielded Faraday chambers, each connected to an EEG. They were given time to 'connect' before isolation. One subject received a random visual stimulus. The paired subject — in complete electromagnetic isolation — showed a correlated EEG response at p < 0.005. The 1994 Physics Essays paper, co-authored with quantum physicist Amit Goswami, proposed that consciousness connects through a non-local field he called 'the Lattice.'

The Bastyr University replication (Standish et al., 2004) used fMRI instead of EEG and produced consistent results. Dean Radin's 2005 meta-analysis of 25 similar studies found a modest but consistent effect size — not the signature of systematic methodological artifacts.

The CIA angle is documented, not speculative: Grinberg's transferred-potential work appears in declassified CIA documents related to Project Stargate. His work was read and filed by American intelligence agencies studying non-local consciousness research.

Four months after publishing the Physics Essays paper, he vanished. His computers were removed from his home. His research files were removed. His wife was named a suspect. The lead investigator who challenged her account was expelled from the police force. No body has been found in 32 years.

We can document what we know. We can't verify what we suspect. The gap between those two things is where the Grinberg case lives.

Full case file: https://www.blackswanp.com/research/scientists/jacobo-grinberg

slides.title: SLIDE 1 — HOOK

slides.body: Grinberg 1994, Physics Essays: two meditators in separate Faraday cages, no physical connection. When one received a visual stimulus, the other showed a correlated EEG response — p < 0.005.

slides.title: SLIDE 3 — REVEAL
slides.body: The CIA filed his work. Grinberg's transferred-potential research appears in declassified Project Stargate documents. American intelligence agencies were reading him. He was aware of Stargate and cited it in his own writing.

slides.title: SLIDE 4 — DETAIL
slides.body: The Bastyr University replication (Standish et al., 2004): fMRI instead of EEG. Correlated brain activity between physically isolated pairs. Statistically significant. Dean Radin's 2005 meta-analysis of 25 similar studies found a consistent effect.

slides.title: SLIDE 5 — QUOTE
slides.body: "Something happened. Someone wanted his work gone. That's not a conspiracy theory. That's the documented record." — Black Swan Project analysis of the Grinberg case

slides.title: SLIDE 6 — IMPLICATION
slides.body: 32 years. No body. No resolution. His computers and research files were removed. The lead investigator was expelled. We can't say why. We can say it happened.

reddit.title: Jacobo Grinberg — the UNAM neurophysiologist who published non-local EEG correlations in 1994, then vanished with his research files removed
reddit.body:
This case doesn't come up as often as it should, so here's a summary of what's actually documented.

Who he was: Jacobo Grinberg-Zylberbaum, PhD neurophysiology from UNAM, postdoc at NYU. Founded the Instituto Nacional para el Estudio de la Conciencia in 1987. Published peer-reviewed papers and 50+ books. Ran experimental protocols most Western neuroscience departments wouldn't touch.

The experiment: Two meditators placed in separate electromagnetically shielded Faraday chambers with EEG recording. No physical connection, no audio, no visual signal between them. After a period of 'connection,' they were isolated. One subject received a random visual stimulus — predictable EEG response documented. The paired subject showed a correlated EEG response. Published p-value: < 0.005. Paper: 'The Einstein-Podolsky-Rosen Paradox in the Brain: The Transferred Potential,' Physics Essays, 1994, co-authored with quantum physicist Amit Goswami.

Replication: Leanna Standish at Bastyr University (2004) used fMRI instead of EEG, documented correlated brain activity between isolated pairs at statistically significant levels. Radin (2005) meta-analyzed 25 similar studies — modest but consistent combined effect, not a methodological artifact signature.

CIA connection: Grinberg's work appears in declassified Project Stargate CIA documents. He was aware of Stargate and referenced it in his own writing. The nature of any formal relationship between him and U.S. intelligence has never been clarified.

The disappearance: December 8, 1994 — four months after the Physics Essays paper. Computers removed from his home. Research files removed. Wife named as suspect. Lead investigator who challenged her account was expelled from the police force. No body. No remains. No credible sighting. 32 years.

Full file: https://www.blackswanp.com/research/scientists/jacobo-grinberg

reddit.title: The Grinberg EEG experiment (1994): what the paper actually showed, what the replications found, and the unresolved disappearance
reddit.body:
For anyone interested in the peer-reviewed side of non-local consciousness research, the Grinberg case is one of the more interesting in the literature.

The experimental design (from the 1994 Physics Essays paper):
- Two meditators in separate Faraday chambers (electromagnetically shielded)
- No physical, auditory, or visual connection between chambers
- Period of intentional 'connection' before isolation
- One subject shown random visual stimuli
- Paired subject's EEG recorded simultaneously
- Result: correlated EEG response in isolated subject at p < 0.005

This isn't a blog post claim. The paper is in Physics Essays, co-authored by UNAM neurophysiologist Jacobo Grinberg-Zylberbaum and quantum physicist Amit Goswami.

Replications:
- Standish et al. (Bastyr University, 2004): fMRI, isolated pairs, statistically significant correlated brain activity
- Radin (2005): meta-analysis of 25 similar studies, consistent modest effect size across studies — not the pattern you get from systematic artifact

The honest position: the result is documented, replicated once at a peer-reviewed level, and has a consistent effect direction across a meta-analysis. It doesn't prove non-local consciousness in any strong sense. It proves a documented anomaly that warrants controlled investigation.

The disappearance: Grinberg vanished December 8, 1994. His research files were removed from his home. The investigator who challenged the prime suspect's account was expelled from the force. 32 years: no resolution.

Full documented file: https://www.blackswanp.com/research/scientists/jacobo-grinberg

email.subject: He published the evidence. Then he vanished.
email.preheader: 32 years. No body. His research files were removed. The investigator was expelled. The CIA had his papers.
email.body:
In 1994, Jacobo Grinberg-Zylberbaum published a peer-reviewed paper in Physics Essays: two meditators in separate Faraday cages, no physical connection, and a correlated EEG response when one received a visual stimulus — p < 0.005.

Four months later, he vanished from his home near Mexico City. His computers were removed. His research files were removed. The lead investigator who challenged the prime suspect was expelled from the police force.

32 years later: no body. No resolution.

The Bastyr University replication (2004) used fMRI and found consistent results. His work appeared in declassified CIA Project Stargate documents. He was aware of Stargate. The nature of any formal relationship with U.S. intelligence has never been clarified.

We can document what we know. We can't verify what we suspect.

Full case file: https://www.blackswanp.com/research/scientists/jacobo-grinberg
